data.title: ChatGPT Prompts for Content Writing
data.metaTitle: 15 Best ChatGPT Prompts for Content Writing (Copy-Paste) 2026
data.metaDescription: Use these ChatGPT prompts for content writing to write blog posts, articles, and landing pages 3x faster. Includes blog post structure, headline generation, and intro formulas.
data.keywords: chatgpt prompts for content writing, content writing prompts chatgpt, ai prompts for blog writing, chatgpt blog post prompts
data.heading: 15 ChatGPT Prompts for Content Writing That Actually Work
data.intro: Most ChatGPT content writing prompts produce generic output. These prompts are engineered to produce content that sounds human, matches your brand voice, and ranks in search engines.

prompts.title: Blog post outline (full structure)
prompts.prompt: Write a detailed blog post outline for "[TOPIC]" targeting the keyword "[KEYWORD]". Include: a hook-driven introduction, 5-7 H2 headings with 2-3 H3 subpoints each, a data/stat section, a common objections section, and a conclusion with CTA. Target length: 1,800 words. Audience: [AUDIENCE].
prompts.tip: Replace [TOPIC], [KEYWORD], and [AUDIENCE] with your specifics. The more precise you are, the less editing you'll need.

prompts.title: Introduction paragraph (hook formula)
prompts.prompt: Write 3 different introduction paragraphs for a blog post about "[TOPIC]". Use a different hook for each: (1) a surprising statistic, (2) a relatable problem/pain point, (3) a bold contrarian claim. Each intro should be 60-80 words and end with a sentence that makes the reader want to read on.
prompts.tip: Test all 3 intros. The contrarian version usually performs best on social shares.

prompts.title: Headline generator (10 variations)
prompts.prompt: Generate 10 headline variations for a blog post about "[TOPIC]". Include: 2 how-to headlines, 2 list headlines, 2 question headlines, 2 benefit-driven headlines, and 2 curiosity-gap headlines. Target keyword: "[KEYWORD]". Keep all under 65 characters for SEO.
prompts.tip: Use Headline Analyzer to score each one. Aim for 70+ score before publishing.

prompts.title: Conclusion with CTA
prompts.prompt: Write a conclusion for a blog post about "[TOPIC]". The conclusion should: (1) summarize the 3 most important takeaways in 2 sentences each, (2) reinforce the main benefit, (3) end with a clear CTA that links to "[YOUR PRODUCT/SERVICE]". Tone: [TONE]. Keep it under 150 words.
prompts.tip: Strong conclusions reduce bounce rate significantly. The CTA placement here is one of the highest-converting spots.

prompts.title: SEO meta description
prompts.prompt: Write 3 meta descriptions for a blog post titled "[TITLE]". Each must: be under 155 characters, include the keyword "[KEYWORD]", have a clear benefit or curiosity hook, and end with a subtle CTA. Number them 1-3.
prompts.tip: Meta descriptions directly affect CTR. Test 2 versions in Google Search Console.

data.cta: Get 150 more prompts for content, marketing, and sales
